### Post by tha_rainman on 2006-06-02


### Post by tha_rainman on 2006-06-02
Ok I've managed to get the 1680x1050 resolution after reconfiguring xorg. Now my problem is that my dpi is incorrect. 

```
chandhana@ubuntu:~$ xdpyinfo | grep dimensions
  dimensions:    1680x1050 pixels (431x272 millimeters)
chandhana@ubuntu:~$ xdpyinfo | grep resolution
  resolution:    99x98 dots per inch
```

The last one should be 96x96 dots per inch right? I've added the correct line to the monitor section but somehow its being ignored...

```
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Viewsonic VX2025wm"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-84
	VertRefresh	43-60
**	DisplaySize 	444	277	# 1680x1050 96dpi**
EndSectio
```

What now?

### Post by BoomerAus on 2006-08-06

---

### Post by BoomerAus on 2006-08-06
Ok, finally got it working ok.

Here's my xorg.conf file for anyone with the Viewsonic VX2025wm monitor:

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Viewsonic"
    ModelName      "VX2025wm"
    HorizSync       60.0 - 70.0
    VertRefresh     60.0 - 70.0
    Option         "DPMS"
    # 1680x1050 @ 60.00 Hz (GTF) hsync: 65.22 kHz; pclk: 147.14 MHz
     Modeline "1680x1050_60.00"  147.14  1680 1784 1968 2256  1050 1051 1054 1087 -HSync +Vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BusID	    "PCI:1:0:0"
    Option          "RenderAccel"   "true"
    Option          "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
    Option          "Coolbits" "1"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option "UseEdidFreqs" "False"
    SubSection     "Display"
    Depth       24
    Modes      "1680x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Don't know why it suddenly decided to work as I'm sure I had used this file before with "out of range" error, but it's working now.

---

### Post by dbw on 2006-08-21
BommerAus- what is this "useEdidfreqs" business?

---

### Post by xtknight on 2006-08-21
> **dbw said:**
> BommerAus- what is this "useEdidfreqs" business?

For nvidia drivers that tells them to ignore refresh rates sent by the monitor to the graphics card and instead listen to what frequences ranges you tell it in xorg.conf.  From my experience, normally it should always max out (>60 Hz with LCDs can be a tad problematic) unless you tell it to force 60 Hz.

---
